question.text: Under Section 15H of the SEBI Act, what is the penalty for non-disclosure of acquisition of shares and takeovers?

answer.text: Not less than one lakh rupees, up to one crore rupees
Not less than five lakh rupees, up to ten crore rupees
Not less than ten lakh rupees, up to twenty-five crore rupees or three times the profits made, whichever is higher
Not less than fifty lakh rupees, up to fifty crore rupees

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 15H prescribes a minimum of not less than ten lakh rupees and maximum extending to twenty-five crore rupees or three times the profits made out of such failure, whichever is higher. **The Trap:** The penalty structure mirrors Section 15G (insider trading) in terms of amounts, reflecting the equal seriousness of takeover non-disclosure. **Cross-Exam Context:** Takeover regulation penalties are tested in NISM Series III-A and IX. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Acquirers must ensure timely disclosure of shareholding changes to avoid these substantial penalties.
